steps.title: Read two endpoint values from the line of best fit
steps.content: The line of best fit spans $x=0$ to $x=10$ hours. Reading from the graph, two clear points on the line are approximately $(0,175)$ and $(10,575)$.

steps.title: Use a mini-table regression to get the slope
steps.content: Enter those two points as lists in Desmos and regress $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=40$, so the line predicts an increase of about $40^\circ\text{F}$ per hour.

steps.title: Match the slope and direction to the choices
steps.content: The slope is positive, so the predicted temperature increases, not decreases. Its size is about $40^\circ\text{F}$ per hour, not $170^\circ\text{F}$ per hour. Choice C states both the correct direction and the correct rate.
